resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MCM6/LCT rs4988235 CT call is available for lactase-persistence context. This is not a diagnosis, symptom explanation, food-sensitivity result, vitamin deficiency result, diet recommendation, or supplement recommendation.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker can provide lactose-related context, but symptoms, diet history, labs, environment, medications, and professional review matter for nutrition decisions.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: No calibrated vitamin score, micronutrient model, supplement model, dosing model, child-specific nutrition evidence, symptom context, diet-history context, lab context, or ancestry applicability metadata is supplied with this fixture.
resultRows.sourceLabel: Nutritional genomics review / GWAS Catalog / DTC result limits
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The local report can describe supplied marker context, but it should not make vitamin status, deficiency, supplement, dosing, child-specific, lab, symptom, diet, metabolic, or treatment conclusions without validated evidence.

resultRows.item: Vitamins DNA Wellness public sample PDF
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The public sample PDF shows vitamin deficiency-risk summary rows, top-suggestion cards, report-method pages, and Vitamin A marker preview context for output-format parity. Exact sample-person vitamin genotypes are not visible in the preview.
resultRows.sourceLabel: SelfDecode Vitamins DNA Wellness sample report
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: These rows document Sequencing.com/SelfDecode sample format only. They must not be treated as this local fixture's vitamin status, deficiency risk, diet advice, supplement guidance, lab interpretation, diagnosis, or treatment conclusion.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The public sample summary table shows a typical vitamin A deficiency-risk category.
sampleRows.description: The summary page shows the vitamin A row, and the later Vitamin A preview page links the topic to beta-carotene conversion and retinol transport marker context.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted by OCR and visual review from pages 2 and 11 of the public SelfDecode Vitamins sample PDF; this is observed sample structure, not a local vitamin A result or advice. This is sample-report structure only, not a personal vitamin, diet, supplement, lab, diagnosis, or treatment conclusion.

vitaminContext: Use this report as educational vitamin and nutrient genetics context, not as a supplement or diet plan.
Keep the public Vitamins DNA Wellness sample rows separate from local findings; sample risk labels, suggestions, and marker descriptions are format examples only.
Discuss symptoms, diet history, lab testing, medications, pregnancy status, children, and supplement questions with qualified professionals.
Do not start, stop, or dose supplements based on genotype alone.

missingInputs: Authenticated marketplace detail-page text and full-report pages have not been extracted for this report.
Exact sample-person vitamin genotypes are not visible in the public sample preview.
No calibrated vitamin, micronutrient, supplement, dosing, child-specific nutrition, symptom, diet-history, lab, or ancestry applicability model is supplied with this fixture.
Broader symptoms, food history, diet history, labs, medications, medical history, ancestry applicability, child-specific needs, and professional assessment are not represented in this fixture.
uncertainty: Nutrition, vitamin status, food response, nutrient status, symptoms, and metabolic outcomes depend on genetics plus symptoms, diet history, labs, medical history, medications, environment, ancestry applicability, and professional review.
A single marker should not be used to diagnose food reactions, nutrient deficiency, vitamin deficiency, metabolic disease, or to prescribe diet or supplement changes.
The public sample PDF is exact output-format evidence for visible preview pages, but its risk labels, top suggestions, and marker context are not local-user evidence.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.

The report is educational and does not diagnose vitamin or nutrient deficiency, prescribe supplements, recommend dosing, create child-specific nutrition guidance, interpret labs, or provide treatment guidance.
The Vitamins sample PDF is image-based; OCR rows were visually checked and obvious OCR errors were corrected, but full authenticated detail-page parity remains pending.
